ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
description: A maternity benefit programme providing financial assistance to pregnant women and lactating mothers for their first live birth. The scheme compensates for wage loss so women can rest before and after delivery.
benefits: Cash incentive of ₹5,000 paid in three instalments
First instalment: ₹1,000 on early registration of pregnancy
Second instalment: ₹2,000 after 6 months of pregnancy and at least one antenatal check-up
Third instalment: ₹2,000 after child birth registration and first cycle of vaccination
eligibility: All pregnant women and lactating mothers for first live birth
Women who are not central/state government employees
how_to_apply: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or Health Centre
Fill Form 1-A for the first instalment
Submit Aadhaar card, bank account details, and MCP card

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India
description: A safe motherhood intervention to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality by promoting institutional delivery among poor pregnant women. JSY is a 100% centrally sponsored scheme.
benefits: Cash assistance to pregnant women for delivering in government health institutions
Rural areas — Low Performing States: ₹1,400 per delivery
Urban areas — Low Performing States: ₹1,000 per delivery
Rural areas — High Performing States: ₹700 per delivery
Urban areas — High Performing States: ₹600 per delivery
ASHA incentive: ₹600 in rural and ₹200 in urban areas (Low Performing States)
eligibility: All pregnant women giving birth in government health facilities
Applies for all deliveries regardless of number of children
how_to_apply: Register with the nearest ASHA/ANM worker or PHC
Carry BPL card, Aadhaar card, and MCP card
Deliver in a government hospital or accredited private institution
Cash is paid post-delivery directly to bank account or via ASHA

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India
description: An initiative to provide completely free services to pregnant women and newborns in government health facilities, eliminating out-of-pocket expenses.
benefits: Free and zero expense delivery in government health facilities
Free caesarean section surgery
Free drugs and consumables
Free diagnostics (blood tests, ultrasound)
Free blood transfusion
Free diet during hospital stay (up to 3 days for normal delivery, 7 days for C-section)
Free transport from home to facility and back
Exempted from user charges
Free treatment for sick newborns up to 30 days
eligibility: All pregnant women going for delivery in government facilities
All sick newborns up to 30 days of age accessing public sector facilities
how_to_apply: Simply visit any government health facility for delivery
Present Aadhaar or any government ID if available
No BPL certificate or formal application required

ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
description: Umbrella scheme for women empowerment including safety, security, and support for women and girls. Sambal sub-scheme covers One Stop Centre, Nari Adalat, and legal support. Samarthya covers nutrition and reproductive health.
benefits: Poshan Abhiyaan — nutrition support during pregnancy and lactation
Iron and folic acid supplements
Counseling on maternal and child nutrition
One Stop Centre (OSC) support for women in distress
Skill development and self-help group support
eligibility: All women and girls, prioritizing vulnerable groups
Women facing domestic violence or distress
how_to_apply: Contact the nearest Anganwadi Centre or CDPOs

ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
description: India's flagship programme to reduce malnutrition among children and pregnant/lactating women. Focuses on first 1000 days of life.
benefits: Supplementary nutrition through Anganwadi Centers
Iron, folic acid, Vitamin A, and zinc supplementation
Nutritional counseling for pregnant and lactating mothers
Real-time monitoring of nutritional status
Take-Home Ration (THR) for pregnant and lactating women
eligibility: Lactating mothers up to 6 months post-delivery
how_to_apply: Register at the nearest Anganwadi Centre with your pregnancy details
Supplements and rations given directly at AWC
